theory.intro: A rectangular prism can be seen as a stack of identical layers of unit cubes. The volume is the number of cubes in one layer multiplied by the number of layers.
facts: **Cubes in one layer $=$ length $\times$ width** of that layer.
**Volume $=$ cubes per layer $\times$ number of layers**.
The number of layers is the **height** of the prism in cubes.
This gives the same answer as length $\times$ width $\times$ height.
steps: Count the cubes in one layer (length $\times$ width).
Count how many layers are stacked (the height).
Multiply the two numbers to get the total volume.
